compiled_state.title: compute-diff-hash.sh takes ~88s in merge-mode — computes merge-base diffs for large changesets, causing test-gate timeout
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

The compute-diff-hash.sh script takes ~88 seconds when running in merge-mode (MERGE_HEAD present) because it computes merge-base diffs for the full changeset. This alone exceeds the 10s test-gate timeout by 8x, making the test-gate fast-path optimization (38a0-e706) ineffective for merge commits — even with the fast-path, the diff hash computation alone blocks.

## Root cause

compute-diff-hash.sh in merge-mode runs git diff operations against the merge base to produce a deterministic hash of the staged changes. For large merges (31+ files with significant diffs), this git diff operation takes ~88s. The hash computation is the bottleneck, not the per-file fuzzy matching.

## Impact

- Test-gate fast-path (Fix 1 for 38a0-e706) will NOT help for merge commits because the diff hash computation itself exceeds the timeout
- This is a distinct problem from the per-file fuzzy matching slowness
- Merge commits are effectively blocked from using the fast-path

## Expected behavior

The diff hash computation should complete within the 10s timeout budget. For merge commits, either:
1. Use a cheaper hash computation (e.g., hash the staged file list + stat info instead of full diffs)
2. Cache the hash from record-test-status.sh so the pre-commit hook doesn't need to recompute it
3. Skip the merge-base diff and hash only the staged content (git diff --cached)

## Fix needed

Optimize compute-diff-hash.sh for merge commits. The hash must still be deterministic and cover all staged changes (to prevent stale status reuse), but it should not require computing full merge-base diffs.

comments.body: INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED: safeguard_approval | Behavioral code bug: compute-diff-hash.sh timeout. Fix requires editing plugins/dso/scripts/compute-diff-hash.sh (safeguard). Re-run in interactive session to approve.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Cluster with 3bd1-6c02 (both about compute-diff-hash.sh performance).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Investigation RESULT: ROOT_CAUSE: 3 compounding bottlenecks — config-paths.sh spawns 7 subprocesses via read-config.sh, merge-mode iterates per-file to build pathspec arrays, and compute-diff-hash is invoked 2+ times per commit. CONFIDENCE: high. FIX: (1) Cache config-paths.sh results, (2) optimize merge-mode diff, (3) add cross-hook hash caching.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
